title: High-resolution terrain for precision artillery matches
intent: Increase match quality and strategic depth by generating high-resolution terrain silhouettes that look natural, play fairly, and support precise shot outcomes and crater feedback in every multiplayer match.

scenarios.description: A newly created match always starts with terrain that is visibly varied (ridges, valleys, flat pockets) and both players are placed on stable, playable ground.
Given the same seed and command sequence, terrain shape and all terrain deformation outcomes are identical across replay and verification runs.
Projectile impacts produce smooth, local terrain deformation so players can reliably use crater edges and line-of-sight changes for follow-up shots.
High-resolution terrain does not harm core UX: match start remains fast and turns remain responsive under expected concurrent matches.

riskNotes: Risk: added terrain detail can introduce unfair spawn states or slower turn latency. Mitigation: enforce spawn/playability constraints, deterministic replay checks, and strict performance budgets before promotion.
